text.title: Architect Mentoring Curriculum Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Architect Mentoring Curriculum methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Curriculum applies to one candidate at a time; cohort delivery hides individual gap closure.
rule.rationale: Per-candidate calibration is the value; cohort delivery loses the per-gap feedback loop.

rule.statement: Curriculum is built against the architect-level rubric; without a rubric, graduation is opinion.
rule.rationale: Promotion-by-opinion drifts toward favorites; rubric-anchored promotion is the only auditable form.

rule.statement: Each candidate has a primary + secondary mentor; single-mentor pairings are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-mentor pairings inherit the mentor's blind spots wholesale; the secondary catches them.

rule.statement: Curriculum carries semver + last_reviewed; stale curricula re-audited each year.
rule.rationale: Architecture practice evolves; curricula must follow or they train candidates for last year's job.

rule.statement: Monthly progress review MUST score against ≥3 rubric items; sub-rubric reviews are deferred sponsor judgment.
rule.rationale: Sub-rubric reviews lose the calibration; full reviews keep promotion decisions defensible.
